steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: What do you say when you see a friend?
What do you say at the end of a chat?
questions.hint: It's a way to end a conversation nicely.

questions.question_text: How do you ask someone to join your play?
questions.hint: Use polite words to invite someone.

steps.question_text: Match the emoji to the word: 👋

steps.title: First Day Greeting
steps.text: He says, 'Hello!' She says, 'Hi!' They listen and answer the teacher.

steps.audio_text: She waves and says, 'Hi!' He listens and smiles. They all answer together.

questions.question: What do they all do together?

questions.question_text: Who is it? (Reading comprehension question)
How do you greet someone? (Vocabulary question)
Complete the sentence: She ___ to music. (Grammar question)
True or False: 'Hi' is the same as 'Hello'.
Who says hi? (Reading comprehension question)

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: What color is Ben's backpack?
questions.hint: Look at the backpack Ben is carrying.

questions.question_text: What game do Ben and Kim like to play?
questions.hint: Think about a game with a ball.

questions.question_text: Who loves reading storybooks?

items.definition: Used to refer to two or more people
Not old or not known before
Having existed for a long time

steps.question_text: Match the emoji to the word: 👥

steps.title: Ben and Kim
steps.text: Today, Ben and Kim join our class. They are new. Ben is happy. Kim is shy.

steps.explanation: Use he or she for one person; they for two.

items.correctSentence: They are called Kim and Ben

steps.audio_text: They are friends. Kim is new and her name is Kim. Ben is old but not too old.

steps.prompt: Let's practice saying sentences about friends!
steps.expected_text: Hello, he is called Ben. He is new.

prompts.prompt: Say: He is called Ben. He is new.
prompts.expected_text: He is called Ben. He is new.

questions.question_text: They are ___ friends. (Vocabulary question)
Who is called Ben? (Grammar question)
True or False: Ben is new. (Reading comprehension question)
What is your friend's ___? (Vocabulary question)
Ben is new. Is this correct? (Grammar question)

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: What sound does the first letter of the alphabet make?
questions.hint: It's like the sound at the start of 'apple'!

questions.question_text: Which letter is in the word 'apple'?
questions.hint: It's the same as the first letter of 'alphabet'!

questions.question_text: Which letter comes after 'A'?
questions.hint: It's the second letter of the alphabet!

steps.question_text: Match the emoji to the word: 🔤

steps.title: The Spelling Game
steps.text: In class, they play a spelling game. She spells 'dog'. He spells 'cat'. They clap for the winner!

steps.rule_pattern: ___ is playing the game. Who is ___?

steps.audio_text: She is learning the alphabet. He spells the word 'cat'.

steps.prompt: Let's practice speaking using the alphabet words!

questions.question_text: He ___ playing the game. (Grammar question)
Which word matches '🔤'? (Vocabulary question)
True or False: A 'letter' is a part of the alphabet.
What word does he spell if he spells 'D-O-G'? (Reading comprehension)
Who is ___? (Grammar question)

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: Which word means to say hello?
questions.hint: It's what you do when you meet someone.

questions.question_text: What do you call someone you meet for the first time?
questions.hint: It's someone you don't know yet.

questions.question_text: What is one thing you can say to be polite?
questions.hint: It's a magic word we use to be nice.

steps.title: New Friends
steps.text: They are new. Hello! My name is Tim. Who is she? She is Ana. Let's listen and answer.

steps.audio_text: Hi! I'm Alex and this is my friend, Jane. We like to sing the ABC song together. Can you say your name with us?

questions.question: Who likes to sing the ABC song?

steps.prompt: Let's practice speaking with sentences about you!
steps.expected_text: Hello, my name is Ana and I can spell my name.

prompts.prompt: Say: 'I like to spell my name: A-N-A'
prompts.expected_text: I like to spell my name: A-N-A

questions.question_text: He ___ happy. (Grammar question)
Which emoji shows 'teacher'? (Vocabulary question)
True or False: 'Hello' is a greeting.
Who is new? (Reading comprehension question)
